optimizer_switches.name: materialization
optimizer_switches.explanation: A subquery or derived table is executed once and its result stored in a temporary table for reuse. Watch tmp_table_size / max_heap_table_size — it may spill to disk.

warnings.text: 1 temp table(s) (Materialize) — up to 3000 rows; may spill to disk
1 sort operation(s) — 10 rows; may use disk-based filesort

suggestions.action: Add indexes on filter/join columns to avoid full table scans
Increase tmp_table_size / max_heap_table_size to keep temp tables in memory
Increase sort_buffer_size or add an ordered index to avoid filesort

executive_summary: Query scans 1 table, materializes 1 temp table and sorts the result; examines ~12,001 rows to return 10 in 7.1 ms. Main finding: full scan of agg (3,000 rows).

query.raw: /* select#1 */ select `u`.`name` AS `name`,`agg`.`total_spent` AS `total_spent` from `testdb`.`users` `u` join (/* select#2 */ select `testdb`.`orders`.`user_id` AS `user_id`,sum(`testdb`.`orders`.`total`) AS `total_spent` from `testdb`.`orders` group by `testdb`.`orders`.`user_id`) `agg` where (`u`.`id` = `agg`.`user_id`) order by `agg`.`total_spent` desc limit 10
query.beautified:
select u.name AS name,agg.total_spent AS total_spent
from testdb.users u
  join (/*
select#2 */
select testdb.orders.user_id AS user_id,sum(testdb.orders.total) AS total_spent
from testdb.orders
group by testdb.orders.user_id) agg
where (u.id = agg.user_id)
order by agg.total_spent desc
limit 10
